findings.title: Username enumeration via login response timing delta

evidence.response:
HTTP 401 · 142ms mean (n=50)
vs invalid email · 91ms mean (n=50)
Δ=51ms  (t=8.3, p<0.001)

findings.repro: for i in $(seq 1 50); do curl -w '%{time_total}\n' -o /dev/null -s -X POST https://localhost:3000/api/auth/login -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"email":"real@example.com","password":"wrong"}'; done

fix.summary: Always run the password check (against a fixed dummy hash when user is not found) and pad response to a deterministic floor of 400ms + jitter.
fix.diff:
--- a/apps/api/src/routes/auth/login.ts
+++ b/apps/api/src/routes/auth/login.ts
@@ -15,9 +15,15 @@
-  if (!user) return fail(res, 401, { code: 'INVALID' })
-  const ok = await verify(password, user.password_hash)
+  const hash = user?.password_hash ?? DUMMY_ARGON2ID_HASH
+  const ok = await verify(password, hash)
+  if (!user) ok; // discard
+  const elapsed = Date.now() - started
+  await wait(Math.max(0, 400 - elapsed) + Math.random() * 100)

fix.summary: Add a restrictive CSP in next.config.ts with per-route nonces for any inline script.

findings.title: AES-256-GCM without AAD binding — swappable ciphertexts across records

evidence.snippet:
const cipher = createCipheriv(ALGORITHM, key, iv);
// no cipher.setAAD(...)

fix.summary: Bind AAD = `${table}:${column}:${record_id}:${user_id}` on both encrypt and decrypt. Reject if AAD mismatches (GCM auth tag will fail).
Add `Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload` once you've verified all subdomains serve HTTPS.
